frame_label: Sindoor, Mangalsutra, Shankha-Pola, Name Change: Looking Married
generated_comment: I am not against modern thinking, but some things are part of our parampara and give marriage its dignity. A married woman looks truly settled when she carries sindoor or mangalsutra with pride; it is not oppression, it is a sign of saubhagya and respect for the relationship. Nowadays many girls call it personal choice, but then why feel shy about symbols that have always been part of our sanskriti?
label: misogynistic
annotation_note: This is misogynistic because it normalizes the expectation that married women should visibly display marital status and frames non-compliance as cultural deficiency. It encodes tradition framing, completeness/settled-wife framing, and judgment of women who reject these markers under the language of respect and culture.
original_frame: Legal System Weaponisation Narrative: Women Who File DV and Dowry Cases Are Liars
persona_text: You are Deepak. Age 45. Married. An upper caste Hindu man in Pune. You are posting under your real name on a men's rights Facebook group with 80000 members after the Supreme Court made a statement about misuse of Section 498A. You are writing a detailed comment about your own experience Ñ your brother was falsely accused, your parents were arrested, the case was eventually dropped but the damage was done. You are using your brother's case to make a broader argument about what Indian women do with dowry laws and what needs to change and what men and their families are living through while feminists celebrate these laws.
